planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Clarify the unique versus redundant informational contribution of strongly correlated features with high relative importance.
key_checks: Measure pairwise correlation strength and overlap in predictive signal among top-ranked features
Assess feature importance stability under perturbations that remove or combine correlated features
Compare distributional and dependency profiles of correlated features across classes and partitions
Evaluate whether correlated features exhibit shortcut-like behavior or local label consistency
success_criteria: Evidence distinguishes whether correlated features provide complementary or redundant signals
Identification of any evaluation artifacts inflating importance of correlated features
Bounded understanding of how redundancy impacts feature importance rankings
router_constraints: preserve evidence references e3, e4, e13, e16, e22

planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Determine if the dominant single port in the DDoS class reflects a genuine attack pattern or sampling artifact influenced by duplication and class imbalance.
key_checks: Analyze class-conditioned distribution stability of Destination Port across partitions
Quantify impact of duplicated rows on port distribution skew and entropy measures
Examine relationship between class imbalance and distributional divergence in Destination Port
Check for local consistency and dependency concentration around dominant port values
success_criteria: Clear differentiation between genuine attack pattern and dataset artifact in port distribution
Bounded insight into how duplication and imbalance affect distributional skew
Identification of stable versus unstable distributional signals in Destination Port
router_constraints: preserve evidence references e1, e2, e33

planner_strategies.strategic_objective: Assess the extent to which redundancy among high-salience features may artificially inflate model performance and identify mitigation directions.
key_checks: Evaluate dependency concentration and redundancy patterns among top-ranked features
Test shortcut-like predictive leverage of redundant features individually
Examine local label consistency and distributional overlap in redundant feature clusters
Explore bounded scenarios where removing redundancy affects performance indicators
success_criteria: Evidence bounds the risk of overfitting due to redundant features
Identification of features or clusters that disproportionately contribute to spurious signals
Preliminary directions for mitigating redundancy-driven overfitting risks
router_constraints: preserve evidence references e3, e4, e13, e16, e22
